title: AreaOfInterest
description: AOI variants. Recipes compose their own `oneOf` from the variants they support (radarMosaic uses eeTable + polygon; only the asset recipe allows assetBounds). Smaller AOIs are faster/cheaper in EE; continental-scale AOIs may hit memory/compute limits depending on processing. Prefer the smallest area capturing the user's region.

countryTableId.description: EE asset id of SEPAL's GAUL-derived country/area table. Always use this exact id for country/admin selection.

eeTable.title: AoiEeTable
eeTable.description:
AOI as rows of an EE FeatureCollection. Geometry = union of selected features. Two cases discriminated by `id`:
1. **Country/area** (`id = users/wiell/SepalResources/gaul`) — use `aoi_list_countries` / `aoi_list_country_areas` to obtain the integer ADM_CODE for `key`. Requires `level` ∈ {COUNTRY, AREA}; forces `keyColumn='id'`.
2. **User table** (any other `id`) — filter by `keyColumn` + `key` (both strings) or include all rows (`keyColumn:null, key:null`). `level` not allowed.

`buffer` enlarges the geometry by km — useful for surrounding context (coastline overflow, transboundary features) but raises area roughly proportionally.

id.description:
EE FeatureCollection asset id. Country/area: `users/wiell/SepalResources/gaul`. User table: any FC asset (typically `projects/

/assets/` or `users//`).

keyColumn.description: Column to filter by; `null` = include every row. Must be `'id'` for the country table.

key.description: Match value. Country/area: integer ADM_CODE from FAO GAUL (e.g. 73 Ecuador, 21 Brazil). Resolve via `aoi_list_countries` / `aoi_list_country_areas` — never an ISO alpha-3 string. User table with `keyColumn` set: non-empty string. User table with `keyColumn=null`: must also be `null`.

level.description: Required for country table; forbidden otherwise. `COUNTRY` = whole country geometry; `AREA` = single sub-national area (province/state/district).

buffer.description: Buffer in **kilometers** around the geometry. **Default 0; leave at 0 unless the user explicitly asks to extend the AOI past its boundary.** A buffer is **very expensive** — expands the geometry EE has to process; even a few km can multiply work for a small AOI; large values can push retrievals into hours. Don't volunteer a buffer for coastline overflow, transboundary features, or general safety margin.

if.description: Country/area branch when id is the SEPAL country table.

then.description: Country/area: keyColumn='id', key is integer GAUL ADM_CODE, level required.

else.description: User table: level forbidden; keyColumn and key both set (strings) or both null.

allOf.description: keyColumn and key both set or both null.

polygon.title: AoiPolygon
polygon.description: Hand-drawn polygon for custom regions not matching a country/admin/asset. Coordinate order is `[longitude, latitude]` (GeoJSON, NOT [lat, lng]) in WGS84 decimal degrees. Polygon implicitly closed — don't duplicate the first point. Don't self-intersect.

path.description: Array of `[lng, lat]` points (≥3). lng ∈ [-180, 180], lat ∈ [-90, 90].

assetBounds.title: AoiAssetBounds
assetBounds.description: Use the asset's intrinsic geometry. Only valid for the `asset` recipe (which wraps a single bounding asset).
